Lore Item No Trade
Slot: HEAD
AC: 18
SV COLD: +9 SV MAGIC: +9 SV POISON: +9
WT: 0.6 Size: SMALL

Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Lore:Circlet of Tactics
Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Leather
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):0, 0, 0
Light:Stein of Tears
Stackable:No
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE

IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 08:24:51
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Uncommon
Level to Attain: 40
[Drops | Comments ]

This item is the result of a quest.
Expansion List - Premium only.
Quest Name
Coldain Books of Tactics

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Kael Drakkel
NPC Name
Gkrean Prophet of Tallon


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Kael Drakkel


Uploaded November 27th, 2008
Send a correction
Post Comment
AC does not own at higher levels
QuoteReply
# Nov 10 2001 at 8:09 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361855218
1 post
Lol, ok for monks ac means absolutely crap.. If you are tanking in tov it is already too late.. Only thing you need to worry about is getting all of your resists over 200 at any given point in time.. Other than that leave the ac to MT....
▲ Page top
RE: AC does not own at higher levels
QuoteReply
# Oct 30 2002 at 8:46 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591764595777
92 posts
...put the lid back on the glue pal.

AC is very valuable to monks in many situations, lol.

-- Ashremm Daystar


Edited, Wed Oct 30 20:27:26 2002
▲ Page top
RE: AC does not own at higher levels
QuoteReply
# Sep 11 2002 at 4:35 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591769435794
6 posts
Well I have a 60 retired monk now and just want to let you guys know when your pulling the high end game AC really doesnt matter I have around 1200 ac on my monk and I will still get hit for full most of the time no matter what monks just dont take dmg like warriors so ac doesnt mean much you want to live through just say an easy HoT raid pulling session I put my priority like this HP, Resist, Sta (more HP), then ac. I will say in normal grouping yes AC is important but not when your pulling something that is going to quad you for 400 and a warrior for 375
▲ Page top
RE: AC does not own at higher levels
QuoteReply
# Nov 27 2001 at 7:00 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592758661243
48 posts
Ummm dont we get the hell beat out of us pulling sometimes? Saying AC is crap for monks is like saying wisdom is crap for druids (imho). Any class who takes damage with some regularity would apreciate the added ac.
▲ Page top
RE: AC does not own at higher levels
QuoteReply
# Dec 14 2001 at 8:56 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592245697088
29 posts
Yeah that is a crock.. Yes resist gear is needed for ToV raids.. But I would wear one of these over my tranix crown or froggie crown for soloing or in groups where I am main tanking melee mobs. (I don't solo caster mobs for xp much)

Plus I duo a lot with housemate's cleric - so I tank all the time.

AC is pointless for monks /snicker

Herrick
▲ Page top
RE: AC does not own at higher levels
QuoteReply
# Jun 01 2002 at 6:38 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591776457436
71 posts
In a perfect situation, the one we all tend to i hope, the only 2 peeps who would take hits in a raid would be the puller and the MT.
I'm of course not counting the poor suicidal tasher who's gonna die the first ten seconds of the boss mob fight.Nothing to do to avoid this alas.

I would go further and say that before run speed 3, i'm pretty sure i was taking more hits (including the ones i blocked dodged and avoided of course) than the MT while pulling the junk to clear the path for the serious things.

That's why AC does count for us.

Crimson
Master
Alliance des Seigneurs

Edited, Sat Jun 1 07:30:46 2002
▲ Page top
RE: AC does not own at higher levels
QuoteReply
# Jun 11 2002 at 10:40 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593966206925
29 posts
Saying AC means nothing to a monk obviously proves that you have never gotten your monk past level 10. The thing I love most about my monk is that I can solo with him, even at some higher levels, and soloing without AC is just plain stupid. Also monks sometimes when pulling can take more hits then the tank. AC is very important to monk, and to mine it may be the most important.

Eamonn Foxfists 43 Monk

Karana
▲ Page top
RE: AC does not own at higher levels
QuoteReply
# Jun 15 2002 at 11:37 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1626049033209
51 posts
Plus there is this little Mob ability called Rampage...
▲ Page top
?!?!
QuoteReply
# Sep 15 2001 at 1:45 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591767750634
2 posts
Please, this thing is great for monks...I can't remember the last time I was blasted by cold or heat nukes, seeing as the high level game is in places like Velk's, SG, DN, among others....AC is king in the end.
▲ Page top
2 versions
QuoteReply
# Aug 27 2001 at 9:29 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591759939836
121 posts
there's 2 versions of this. one is quested for, one drops when you kill him.

one of the 2 is not no drop....i imagine it's the one that drops off of him.
▲ Page top
RE: 2 versions
QuoteReply
# May 01 2002 at 6:27 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591763043733
107 posts
most stuff in kael that you can quest or kill the quest giver for is found in two version like you said. the no-drop version is always from killing. and the droppable version is from questing.
▲ Page top
RE: 2 versions
QuoteReply
# Jul 22 2002 at 9:04 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965864169
76 posts
Killed Gkrean prophet last night and won Gaunts of Iron Tactics (same mob that drops this), and the gaunts that dropped are definately droppable. Perhaps you have it backwards?
▲ Page top
Circlet>Frog Crown
QuoteReply
# Jun 02 2001 at 1:10 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
It isn't even questionable if you would trade out a Froglok Crown for this. Circlet is SO much better, the real question is - Is this better than a crown of King Tranix?
▲ Page top
RE: Circlet>Frog Crown
QuoteReply
# Oct 17 2001 at 12:22 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591767500717
12 posts
It is not better then a froggy crown when your hunting in a place like the Halls of Testing in ToV, where you need all the fire/cold resist you can get to resist the AEs. For normal fights, or Magic/Poison AE casting mobs, this is better though.
▲ Page top
great for monks
QuoteReply
# May 14 2001 at 5:56 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
just for the 9mr and extra ac I wear mine everyday ...then for fire mob raids I switch out to froggy crown

Sweet for my monk
▲ Page top
RE: Sweet
QuoteReply
# May 08 2001 at 1:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591756400611
2 posts
Hate to say this, but it isn't that good for a monk. Get a Crown of the Froglok Kings....slightly less AC but 20 Fire and 20 Cold. that helps a whole lot more than sacrificing 11 cold and 20 fire to get 9 magic.....you can get those off of the new rings.

IMHO of course :)

Pedron Bixiebane
Monk of the 49th Fists
▲ Page top
RE: Sweet
QuoteReply
# Feb 14 2002 at 5:44 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1595175385077
23 posts
I am sorry bro, but your wrong. MR is more important then FR any day, especially for a monk. FR is easy to get for us, while MR is quite difficult. I would keep the froggy crown for fire based mobs, but otherwise, this owns it easy.

Shuyung
Epic Monk of the 54th lvl

# Feb 11 2001 at 7:13 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592245625698

Sweet for any melee, especially tanks. I want one with my rogue, though.

# Mar 07 2001 at 2:40 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591753934230

It's nice for any class. Casters will love this one if they don't have an INT boost item, and Clerics will like the added AC, to be sure.
